zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud de Grey於大約1320年出生在英國賀福爾洛--也時，她的父親Henry DeGREY , 3rd Baron Grey de Wilton39歲，母親Anne Rockley32歲。她和John de Lisle至少有3個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1376年在英國越可爾享年57歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Grey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: variant of   Gray  .
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Ukrainian Grushko: nickname or topographic name derived from a Russian-influenced transliteration of grushka ‘pear, pear tree’.

variants.name: Gray
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (especially Eastern Ulster; of Norman origin): habitational name from Graye in Calvados, France, named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Graec(i)us, meaning ‘Greek’ + the locative suffix -acum. This is probably the chief source of the surname in Britain.

English: nickname for someone with gray hair or a gray beard, from Middle English grey (Old English grǣg, grēg) ‘gray’. In Ireland it has been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, gray’, including Mac Giolla Riabhaigh; see McGreevy . In North America, this surname has assimilated names with similar meaning from other languages.
French: habitational name from Gray in Haute-Saône or Le Gray in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: Greiwe
meanings: German: possibly a nickname from Middle High German greibe ‘sharp, tangy, dour’. Compare Grey .

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud de Grey was born about 1320, in Ross-on-Wye, Herefordshire, England, her father, Henry DeGREY , 3rd Baron Grey de Wilton, was 39 and her mother, Anne Rockley, was 32. She had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with John de Lisle. She died in 1376, in Yorkshire, England, at the age of 57.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud de Grey est née environ 1320, à Ross-on-Wye, Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Henry DeGREY , 3rd Baron Grey de Wilton, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Anne Rockley, avait 32 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles avec John de Lisle. Elle est décédée en 1376, à Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 57 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud de Grey um 1320 in Ross-on-Wye, Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Henry DeGREY , 3rd Baron Grey de Wilton 39 und ihre Mutter Anne Rockley 32. Sie hatte mit John de Lisle mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1376 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Yorkshire, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Maud de Grey(이)가 대략 1320년 영국 허퍼드실르 로스--w예에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry DeGREY , 3rd Baron Grey de Wilton(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Rockley(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 John de Lisle(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1376년 영국 요르크실르에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud de Grey родилась около 1320 г. в местности росс-он-ви, хирефордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Henry DeGREY , 3rd Baron Grey de Wilton было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Rockley, 32. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери от John de Lisle. Она умерла в 1376 г. в местности еркшир, Англия в возрасте 57 лет.
